sources.notes: Same easy-version Beautiful Patterns task as 2174C1 under the Div.2 contest entry.
The expected value is handled by expanding the square into pairwise palindromic-substring events.

annotation.summary: Compute the expected square of the number of palindromic substrings in a random color string.
annotation.constraints: n <= 2000 in the easy version, so pairwise event enumeration is intended.
annotation.core_idea: Expand correctness^2 into ordered pairs of substring-palindrome indicators. For each pair, count how many independent color variables remain after enforcing both palindrome equality systems, and sum the corresponding probability modulo p.
annotation.complexity: Polynomial in n for the easy version.

solution_variants.name: pairwise-indicator-expectation
solution_variants.summary: Sum probabilities of pairs of palindrome events.

tags.evidence: The expected square is computed by summing all ordered pairs of indicators.
The probability of each pair depends on counting independent equality components.
Equality constraints can be represented as connected components.
